control-disablements.description: Set to false to prevent the module from creating any resources
control-disablements.name: enabled

control-disablements.description: Namespace, which could be your organization name or abbreviation, e.g. 'eg' or 'cp'
control-disablements.name: namespace

control-disablements.description: Additional tags for appending to tags_as_list_of_maps. Not added to `tags`.
control-disablements.name: additional_tag_map

control-disablements.description:
The naming order of the id output and Name tag.
Defaults to ["namespace", "environment", "stage", "name", "attributes"].
You can omit any of the 5 elements, but at least one must be present.
control-disablements.name: label_order

control-disablements.description:
Limit `id` to this many characters (minimum 6).
Set to `0` for unlimited length.
Set to `null` for default, which is `0`.
Does not affect `id_full`.
control-disablements.name: id_length_limit

control-disablements.default:
{
  "additional_tag_map": {},
  "attributes": [],
  "delimiter": null,
  "enabled": true,
  "environment": null,
  "id_length_limit": null,
  "label_key_case": null,
  "label_order": [],
  "label_value_case": null,
  "name": null,
  "namespace": null,
  "regex_replace_chars": null,
  "stage": null,
  "tags": {}
}
control-disablements.description:
Single object for setting entire context at once.
See description of individual variables for details.
Leave string and numeric variables as `null` to use default value.
Individual variable settings (non-null) override settings in context object,
except for attributes, tags, and additional_tag_map, which are merged.
control-disablements.name: context

control-disablements.description:
Delimiter to be used between `namespace`, `environment`, `stage`, `name` and `attributes`.
Defaults to `-` (hyphen). Set to `""` to use no delimiter at all.
control-disablements.name: delimiter

control-disablements.description:
The letter case of output label values (also used in `tags` and `id`).
Possible values: `lower`, `title`, `upper` and `none` (no transformation).
Default value: `lower`.
control-disablements.name: label_value_case

control-disablements.description: The region that collects AWS Config data for global resources such as IAM
control-disablements.name: global_resource_collector_region

control-disablements.description: The id of the account that is the centralized cloudtrail logging account.
control-disablements.name: central_logging_account

control-disablements.description:
Regex to replace chars with empty string in `namespace`, `environment`, `stage` and `name`.
If not set, `"/[^a-zA-Z0-9-]/"` is used to remove all characters other than hyphens, letters and digits.
control-disablements.name: regex_replace_chars

control-disablements.description:
The letter case of label keys (`tag` names) (i.e. `name`, `namespace`, `environment`, `stage`, `attributes`) to use in `tags`.
Possible values: `lower`, `title`, `upper`.
Default value: `title`.
control-disablements.name: label_key_case

root.description:
A list of finding severity labels used to filter which Security Hub findings
are forwarded to notifications, e.g. `["CRITICAL", "HIGH"]`.

When empty (the default), findings of all severities matching
`cloudwatch_event_rule_pattern_detail_type` are forwarded, preserving prior
behavior. When set, only findings whose `Severity.Label` is in the list are
forwarded.

For the list of valid severity labels, see:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/securityhub/1.0/APIReference/API_Severity.html
root.name: finding_severity_labels

root.description: ID element. Usually used to indicate role, e.g. 'prod', 'staging', 'source', 'build', 'test', 'deploy', 'release'
root.name: stage

root.description:
Set of labels (ID elements) to include as tags in the `tags` output.
Default is to include all labels.
Tags with empty values will not be included in the `tags` output.
Set to `[]` to suppress all generated tags.
**Notes:**
  The value of the `name` tag, if included, will be the `id`, not the `name`.
  Unlike other `null-label` inputs, the initial setting of `labels_as_tags` cannot be
  changed in later chained modules. Attempts to change it will be silently ignored.
root.name: labels_as_tags

root.description:
Additional key-value pairs to add to each map in `tags_as_list_of_maps`. Not added to `tags` or `id`.
This is for some rare cases where resources want additional configuration of tags
and therefore take a list of maps with tag key, value, and additional configuration.
root.name: additional_tag_map

root.description:
The order in which the labels (ID elements) appear in the `id`.
Defaults to ["namespace", "environment", "stage", "name", "attributes"].
You can omit any of the 6 labels ("tenant" is the 6th), but at least one must be present.
root.name: label_order

root.description:
The detail-type pattern used to match events that will be sent to SNS. 

For more information, see:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/events/CloudWatchEventsandEventPatterns.html
root.name: cloudwatch_event_rule_pattern_detail_type

root.description:
Flag to indicate whether a finding aggregator should be created

If you want to aggregate findings from one region, set this to `true`.

For more information, see:
https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/resources/securityhub_finding_aggregator
root.name: finding_aggregator_enabled

root.description:
Linking mode to use for the finding aggregator. 

The possible values are: 
  - `ALL_REGIONS` - Aggregate from all regions
  - `ALL_REGIONS_EXCEPT_SPECIFIED` - Aggregate from all regions except those specified in `var.finding_aggregator_regions`
  - `SPECIFIED_REGIONS` - Aggregate from regions specified in `finding_aggregator_enabled`
root.name: finding_aggregator_linking_mode

root.description:
A list of regions to aggregate findings from. 

This is only used if `finding_aggregator_enabled` is `true`.
root.name: finding_aggregator_regions

root.description:
Terraform regular expression (regex) string.
Characters matching the regex will be removed from the ID elements.
If not set, `"/[^a-zA-Z0-9-]/"` is used to remove all characters other than hyphens, letters and digits.
root.name: regex_replace_chars

root.description: Set to false to prevent the module from creating any resources
root.name: enabled

root.description: ID element. Usually an abbreviation of your organization name, e.g. 'eg' or 'cp', to help ensure generated IDs are globally unique
root.name: namespace

root.description: ID element _(Rarely used, not included by default)_. A customer identifier, indicating who this instance of a resource is for
root.name: tenant

root.description:
Delimiter to be used between ID elements.
Defaults to `-` (hyphen). Set to `""` to use no delimiter at all.
root.name: delimiter

root.description:
ID element. Additional attributes (e.g. `workers` or `cluster`) to add to `id`,
in the order they appear in the list. New attributes are appended to the
end of the list. The elements of the list are joined by the `delimiter`
and treated as a single ID element.
root.name: attributes

root.description:
Additional tags (e.g. `{'BusinessUnit': 'XYZ'}`).
Neither the tag keys nor the tag values will be modified by this module.
root.name: tags

root.description:
Limit `id` to this many characters (minimum 6).
Set to `0` for unlimited length.
Set to `null` for keep the existing setting, which defaults to `0`.
Does not affect `id_full`.
root.name: id_length_limit

root.default:
{
  "additional_tag_map": {},
  "attributes": [],
  "delimiter": null,
  "descriptor_formats": {},
  "enabled": true,
  "environment": null,
  "id_length_limit": null,
  "label_key_case": null,
  "label_order": [],
  "label_value_case": null,
  "labels_as_tags": [
    "unset"
  ],
  "name": null,
  "namespace": null,
  "regex_replace_chars": null,
  "stage": null,
  "tags": {},
  "tenant": null
}
root.description:
Single object for setting entire context at once.
See description of individual variables for details.
Leave string and numeric variables as `null` to use default value.
Individual variable settings (non-null) override settings in context object,
except for attributes, tags, and additional_tag_map, which are merged.
root.name: context

root.description:
Controls the letter case of the `tags` keys (label names) for tags generated by this module.
Does not affect keys of tags passed in via the `tags` input.
Possible values: `lower`, `title`, `upper`.
Default value: `title`.
root.name: label_key_case

root.description:
Flag to indicate whether an SNS topic should be created for notifications

If you want to send findings to a new SNS topic, set this to true and provide a valid configuration for subscribers
root.name: create_sns_topic

root.description:
The ARN for an SNS topic to send findings notifications to. This is only used if create_sns_topic is false.

If you want to send findings to an existing SNS topic, set the value of this to the ARN of the existing topic and set 
create_sns_topic to false.
root.name: imported_findings_notification_arn

root.description: ID element. Usually used for region e.g. 'uw2', 'us-west-2', OR role 'prod', 'staging', 'dev', 'UAT'
root.name: environment

root.description:
ID element. Usually the component or solution name, e.g. 'app' or 'jenkins'.
This is the only ID element not also included as a `tag`.
The "name" tag is set to the full `id` string. There is no tag with the value of the `name` input.
root.name: name

root.description:
Controls the letter case of ID elements (labels) as included in `id`,
set as tag values, and output by this module individually.
Does not affect values of tags passed in via the `tags` input.
Possible values: `lower`, `title`, `upper` and `none` (no transformation).
Set this to `title` and set `delimiter` to `""` to yield Pascal Case IDs.
Default value: `lower`.
root.name: label_value_case

root.description:
Describe additional descriptors to be output in the `descriptors` output map.
Map of maps. Keys are names of descriptors. Values are maps of the form
`{
   format = string
   labels = list(string)
}`
(Type is `any` so the map values can later be enhanced to provide additional options.)
`format` is a Terraform format string to be passed to the `format()` function.
`labels` is a list of labels, in order, to pass to `format()` function.
Label values will be normalized before being passed to `format()` so they will be
identical to how they appear in `id`.
Default is `{}` (`descriptors` output will be empty).
root.name: descriptor_formats

root.description: Flag to indicate whether default standards should be enabled
root.name: enable_default_standards

root.description:
A list of standards/rulesets to enable

See https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/resources/securityhub_standards_subscription#argument-reference

The possible values are:

  - standards/aws-foundational-security-best-practices/v/1.0.0
  - ruleset/cis-aws-foundations-benchmark/v/1.2.0
  - standards/pci-dss/v/3.2.1
root.name: enabled_standards

root.description: Required configuration for subscibres to SNS topic.
root.name: subscribers
root.type:
map(object({
    protocol = string
    # The protocol to use. The possible values for this are: sqs, sms, lambda, application. (http or https are partially supported, see below) (email is an option but is unsupported, see below).
    endpoint = string
    # The endpoint to send data to, the contents will vary with the protocol. (see below for more information)
    endpoint_auto_confirms = bool
    # Boolean indicating whether the end point is capable of auto confirming subscription e.g., PagerDuty (default is false)
    raw_message_delivery = bool
    # Boolean indicating whether or not to enable raw message delivery (the original message is directly passed, not wrapped in JSON with the original message in the message property) (default is false)
  }))

control-disablements.description: A list of controls to disable based on the the input variables
control-disablements.name: controls

root.description: A list of subscriptions that have been enabled
root.name: enabled_subscriptions

root.description: The SNS topic that was created
root.name: sns_topic

root.description: The SNS topic that was created
root.name: sns_topic_subscriptions
